title: galium odoratum
intro: Galium odoratum, also known as wood sorrel or sweet woodruff, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. Its leaves and roots have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation. The plant is rich in antioxidants, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing stress and improving cardiovascular health. Wood sorrel can be consumed as a tea or added to salads, and its leaves can also be used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves.
